meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Ireland to demand Israel pay for settler demolition of Palestinian school
items.summary: Ireland will demand that Israeli authorities repay the cost of a school that was demolished by settlers.

items.title: West Bank scepticism as Palestinians doubt local elections will change much
items.summary: Frustration with the Palestinian Authority and Israeli occupation fuel voter apathy in local West Bank elections.

items.title: US sanctions China’s ‘teapot’ refinery for buying Iranian oil
items.summary: The Hengli refinery has generated hundreds of millions of dollars for Iran's military, says US Treasury.

items.title: Brazil and Chelsea star Estevao’s World Cup hopes in doubt after injury
items.summary: The winger will miss the rest of the season after suffering a hamstring injury in last week's 1-0 loss ⁠to Man United.

items.title: Capture of ships by US, Iran violates international law, shipping body says
items.summary: US and Iran should immediately release crews of captured vessels, says International Chamber of Shipping director.

items.title: Lakers down Rockets in overtime for 3-0 series lead, Celtics beat Sixers
items.summary: Elsewhere in the NBA playoffs, Spurs beat Trail Blazers 120-108 without Victor Wembanyama to take a 2-1 series lead.

items.title: Elections without sovereignty: What Palestine’s local vote really represent
items.summary: The elections offer participation without power, revealing how occupation shapes and limits Palestinian political life.

items.title: What’s at stake for Iran and the US in Islamabad?
items.summary: Iran’s foreign minister is in Islamabad, with US envoys also on the way.

items.title: Israel escalates attacks in Gaza, killing 12 people
items.summary: Israeli forces have killed 12 Palestinians, including six police officers, in multiple attacks across Gaza.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 57 as Trump dispatches negotiating team?
items.summary: US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel to Pakistan, as Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad.

items.title: OpenAI’s Sam Altman apologises over failure to report Canadian mass shooter
items.summary: Tech firm suspended mass shooter's ChatGPT account before attacks, but did not inform law enforcement.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran’s FM in Islamabad; US says envoys to travel for talks
items.summary: Israeli air strikes, tank shelling in Gaza killed at least 12 Palestinians, including six police officers.

items.title: Police raid Peru’s election authorities after outcry over slow vote count
items.summary: Anticorruption police gathered material from the homes of ex-election office head Piero Corvetto and other officials.

items.title: Israel kills at least 12 Palestinians in Gaza amid ‘ceasefire’
items.summary: Hamas says the Israeli escalation represents the failure of the international community to uphold the truce in Gaza.

items.title: Real Madrid stunned at Betis as Barcelona have chance to move 11 clear
items.summary: Ex-Barcelona star Hector Bellerin's injury-time leveller for Betis stunts Real Madrid's Liga title title challenge.

items.title: US sending envoys to Pakistan, raising hopes of talks with Iran’s Araghchi
items.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrives in Islamabad, but Tehran yet to commit to more talks with US delegation.

items.title: Mass wedding for 300 brides and grooms in central Gaza
items.summary: A festive moment in Gaza as a charity-sponsored mass wedding ceremony for 300 brides and grooms in Deir el-Balah.

items.title: Who’s in control of AI?
items.summary: Owner of US tech giant reveals breach of one of world's most powerful AI models.

items.title: Israel continues attacks on Lebanon despite extension of ceasefire
items.summary: Israeli forces killed six Hezbollah fighters in an exchange of fire in the area of Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon.

items.title: Trump to again end legal status of people who entered US with CBP One app
items.summary: Judge had previously blocked move to end temporary legal status for those who entered US via Biden-era application.

items.title: Petro becomes first president to visit Venezuela since Maduro abduction
items.summary: Border security set to loom large as Colombia's leader meets interim Venezuelan President Delcy Rodriguez.

items.title: US appeals court rejects Trump’s ban on asylum seekers, teeing up appeal
items.summary: Judges say Trump's order for swift removal at the border 'cast aside federal laws affording' right to seek asylum.

items.title: Can Europe help the Middle East resolve the war on Iran?
items.summary: Al Jazeera’s Rory Challands reports from the EU summit in Cyprus.

items.title: Trump government extends Jones Act waiver by 90 days to dampen oil prices
items.summary: Move is part of a broader US push to curb politically sensitive fuel price spikes before November’s midterm elections.

items.title: UK assisted dying bill fails after delays but advocates vow to try again
items.summary: Supporters of bill to legalise assisted dying for terminally ill people say they will try again to pass legislation.
